body: {"rationale":"Strong bead overall — title is imperative and names the subsystem, root cause cites three file:line anchors, AC is numbered with Test* names, the go test -run filter and lefthook gate are explicit, labels carry both adr:023 and spec:FEAT-010 cross-refs, parent and dep are set, no /tmp or chat references. One criterion is borderline: the canonical readiness JSON shape is never defined in the bead body. AC 2 says 'canonical readiness JSON decodes into the same pre-claim decision model' and AC 4 references 'fail-open/skip behavior according to ADR-023/FEAT-010 policy' but neither excerpts the discriminator field, the reason-code enumeration, nor the top-level schema fields the agent must implement. A sub-agent working cold must locate and interpret FEAT-010 + ADR-023 to know what to write, which is out-of-spec for standalone sufficiency (P7). The custom_fields.spec-id also names ADR-023 while the governing feature spec is FEAT-010 — a minor inconsistency that could send the agent to the wrong doc first.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["Inline the canonical readiness JSON schema (at minimum the discriminator field name and the valid reason-code set) into the description body, so the agent does not have to locate FEAT-010 to know what to parse.","Inline the relevant ADR-023/FEAT-010 policy sentence for system_unready → fail-open/skip directly into AC 4, e.g. 'system_unready must produce a skip decision (bead remains queued, no error exit) per FEAT-010 §3.2'.","Change custom_fields.spec-id from 'ADR-023' to 'FEAT-010' since FEAT-010 is the governing feature spec; ADR-023 is already captured in the adr:023 label."],"waivers_applied":[]}
